what: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: plain terms: US Census Bureau official source many government’s hard economic numbers — much shops selling, many new houses started, many new businesses formed, much country trades rest world. Several figures move markets moment published. package fetches official statistics directly Census Bureau hands back clean tables can work R, also reaches Bureau’s population demographic surveys (income, employment, lives ). Looking data exists needs sign-; pulling numbers needs free key. fetches tidies figures faithfully leaves interpretation .

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: R API wrapper US Census Bureau Data API supporting synchronous asynchronous (promise based) operations. Provides R6 classes Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) family American Community Survey (ACS) demographic aggregates, plus keyless dataset, variable, geography discovery, built shared connectcore transport base.

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: US Census Bureau runs one largest free public-data APIs world. Beyond every-ten-years population count publishes government’s economic indicators — retail sales, business formation, durable-goods orders, housing starts, international trade — several genuine market-moving prints release day. package gives series tidy data.tables, lets introspect ~1,790-dataset catalogue API key. faithful, low-level wrapper: speaks Bureau’s peculiar wire format (JSON array--arrays whose first row names columns) returns clean tables, editorialise data.

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: data.table everywhere, list columns. Every method returns one flat data.table; measurement columns typed nullable, structural columns strict. Sync async. Every request-making surface works modes. async = TRUE returns [promise][promises::promise]; otherwise table returned directly. single sync/async branch point (inherited connectcore). Keyless discovery. catalogue, per-dataset variable dictionaries, geography hierarchies need key, can validate query spending keyed call. Typed errors. Every failure classed condition (census_api_error, census_validation_error) carrying structured fields — branch type, never grep message.

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: project uses renv. Add census lockfile restore:
code: renv::install("dereckscompany/census") # or, without renv: # remotes::install_github("dereckscompany/census")

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: Data queries require free Census API key; discovery endpoints . Request one https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html, store .Renviron: newly issued key must activated via confirmation email data queries succeed. API redirects data queries HTML error page, package surfaces typed census_api_error naming activation requirement (metadata endpoints keep working throughout).
code: CENSUS_API_KEY="your-40-char-key"

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: Every EITS program queried way: required time predicate (year, month, "... ..." range), optionally narrowed category_code, data_type_code, seasonally_adj. result long tidy — one row per (period × category × data_type × adjustment). convenience wrappers pin program document release cadence: get_retail_advance(), get_durable_goods_advance(), get_housing_starts() round market-moving set.
code: eits <- CensusEconomicIndicators$new()  marts <- eits$get_series("marts", time = "2023") marts #>    program category_code data_type_code seasonally_adj   datetime #>     <char>        <char>         <char>         <char>     <POSc> #> 1:   marts         44000             SM             no 2023-01-01 #> 2:   marts         44000             SM            yes 2023-01-01 #> 3:   marts         44Y72          MPCSM            yes 2023-01-01 #> 4:   marts         44000             SM            yes 2023-02-01 #>    time_slot_name cell_value error_data geo_level #>            <char>      <num>     <char>    <char> #> 1:        Jan2023         NA         no        us #> 2:        Jan2023   612345.0         no        us #> 3:        Jan2023       12.3        yes        us #> 4:        Feb2023   618900.0         no        us bfs <- eits$get_business_formation(time = "2024") bfs #>    program category_code data_type_code seasonally_adj   datetime #>     <char>        <char>         <char>         <char>     <POSc> #> 1:     bfs         TOTAL          BA_BA             no 2024-01-01 #> 2:     bfs         TOTAL          BA_BA             no 2024-02-01 #> 3:     bfs         TOTAL          BA_BA             no 2024-03-01 #>    time_slot_name cell_value error_data geo_level #>            <char>      <num>     <char>    <char> #> 1:        Jan2024     500000         no        us #> 2:        Feb2024     460000         no        us #> 3:        Mar2024     471200         no        us

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: Introspect catalogue dataset’s metadata key:
code: datasets <- census_datasets() datasets[, .(program_path, is_timeseries, is_microdata)]  vars <- census_variables("timeseries/eits/marts") vars[, .(name, label, required)]  census_geographies("2023/acs/acs1") #>             program_path is_timeseries is_microdata #>                   <char>        <lgcl>       <lgcl> #> 1: timeseries/eits/marts          TRUE        FALSE #> 2:         2023/acs/acs1         FALSE        FALSE #> 3:    2024/cps/basic/jan         FALSE         TRUE #>              name                        label required #>            <char>                       <char>   <lgcl> #> 1:     cell_value                   data value     TRUE #> 2:  category_code                Industry list     TRUE #> 3: time_slot_date               Time Slot Date    FALSE #> 4:            for Census API FIPS 'for' clause    FALSE #>    geo_level               name     requires #>       <char>             <char>       <char> #> 1:       010                 us         <NA> #> 2:       040              state         <NA> #> 3:       050             county        state #> 4:       060 county subdivision state;county

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: ACS gives income, employment, demographics every US geography, wide table — one row per geography, one column per requested variable. Estimate (*E) margin--error (*M) columns numeric; annotation code columns character. survey year dataset (time predicate), multi-year pull iterates years stacks year column — year Bureau never released (e.g. 1-year 2020) skipped: wide columns terse variable codes; look labels keyless dictionary: whole grammar validates geography requires chains spending keyed call: wildcard child (county:*) may omit parents, specific one (county:037) must supply — mirroring API exactly.
code: acs <- CensusACS$new()  ca <- acs$get_acs(     2023, "acs1",     variables = c("NAME", "B01001_001E", "B19013_001E"),     geo_for = "county:*", geo_in = "state:06" ) ca #>                      name b01001_001e b19013_001e b19013_001m b19013_001ea #>                    <char>       <num>       <num>       <num>       <char> #> 1: Alpha County, Testland      100000       65000         500         <NA> #> 2:  Beta County, Testland      250000       82000        1200         <NA> #> 3: Gamma County, Testland        5000          NA          NA         <NA> #> 4: Delta County, Testland        1200  -666666666  -555555555         <NA> #>     state county #>    <char> <char> #> 1:     90    001 #> 2:     90    003 #> 3:     90    005 #> 4:     90    007 income <- census_backfill_acs(     from = 2018, to = 2023, dataset = "acs1",     variables = c("NAME", "B19013_001E"), geo_for = "state:*" ) census_acs_labels(2023, "acs1", variables = c("B01001_001E", "B19013_001E"))[, .(name, label)] #>           name                                                   label #>         <char>                                                  <char> #> 1: B01001_001E                                        Estimate!!Total: #> 2: B19013_001E Estimate!!Median household income in the past 12 months

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: Set async = TRUE consume promise coro::async / await, driving event loop later:
code: box::use(coro, later)  eits_async <- CensusEconomicIndicators$new(async = TRUE)  main <- coro::async(function() {     marts <- await(eits_async$get_series("marts", time = "2024"))     bfs <- await(eits_async$get_series("bfs", time = "2024"))     print(list(marts = marts, bfs = bfs)) })  main() while (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now()

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: census_backfill_series() pages time predicate across range years, deduplicates, returns one tidy series:
code: retail <- census_backfill_series(     "marts",     from = 2015, to = 2024,     category_code = "44000", data_type_code = "SM", seasonally_adj = "yes" )

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
text: Census API returns latest revised value period — vintage/-parameter. Retail sales, business formation, construction heavily revised, naive historical pull current-vintage look-ahead trap backtest. Source revisable history ALFRED, snapshot forward pulls fetch time; never feed API’s revised history point--time model.

title: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API
code: result <- tryCatch(     eits$get_series("not_a_program", time = "2024"),     census_validation_error = function(e) paste("caught:", conditionMessage(e)) ) result #> [1] "caught: Invalid EITS program 'not_a_program'. Valid programs: bfs, marts, mrts, mwts, mtis, m3, advm3, ftd, ftdadv, resconst, ressales, vip, qss, hv."

title: American Community Survey (ACS) datasets — ACS_DATASETS
text: two ACS aggregate datasets addressable /data/{year}/acs/<code>. 1-year survey (acs1) covers geographies least 65,000 population; 5-year survey (acs5) covers every geography block group. survey year dataset (time predicate); vintage path's {year} segment.

title: American Community Survey (ACS) datasets — ACS_DATASETS
code: ACS_DATASETS

title: American Community Survey (ACS) datasets — ACS_DATASETS
text: named list scalar<character> path segments: acs1, acs5.

what: CensusACS: American Community Survey aggregates — CensusACS
title: CensusACS: American Community Survey aggregates — CensusACS
text: Retrieves American Community Survey (ACS) estimates — income, employment, demographics every US geography — wide table, one row per geography one column per requested variable.

title: CensusACS: American Community Survey aggregates — CensusACS
text: ACS uses standard get//grammar. choose variables (50 per call; e.g. "B19013_001E" median household income, "NAME" geography label) geography (geo_for, e.g. "county:*"), supplying required parent geographies geo_in (e.g. "state:06"). result dynamic-width AcsTable: estimate (*E) margin (*M) columns numeric, annotation (*EA/*MA) code columns character. Two datasets: acs1 (1-year, geographies least 65,000 population) acs5 (5-year, every geography block group). survey year dataset — vintage year argument, time predicate (unlike EITS family). Note Bureau release standard 1-year estimates 2020. Geography validation: validate_geo = TRUE (default), method makes keyless pre-flight fetch dataset's geography metadata checks requires chain — wildcard child (county:*) may omit parents, specific child (county:037) may . Set validate_geo = FALSE skip pre-flight (census_backfill_acs() , validating ). Inherits CensusBase; methods honour async flag.

title: CensusACS: American Community Survey aggregates — CensusACS
text: ACS annual long lag (1-year ~9 months, 5-year ~15 months) structural/reference series, time signal — see CensusEconomicIndicators vintage discipline applies revisable economic series.

title: CensusACS: American Community Survey aggregates — CensusACS
text: connectcore::RestClient -> census::CensusBase -> CensusACS

title: CensusACS: American Community Survey aggregates — CensusACS
text: year (scalar<count [2005, Inf[>) ACS vintage year. dataset (scalar<character>) ACS dataset, "acs1" "acs5". variables (vector<character, 1..>) get= variables (max 50), e.g. c("NAME", "B01001_001E", "B19013_001E"). geo_for (scalar<character>) geography clause (wire key ), e.g. "county:*"; default "us". geo_in (scalar<character> | NULL) clause (wire key ) supplying required parent geographies, space-separated, e.g. "state:06". Default NULL. validate_geo (scalar<logical>) TRUE (default), keyless pre-flight validates geo_for/geo_in dataset's requires chain keyed call.

title: CensusACS: American Community Survey aggregates — CensusACS
text: (data.table | promise<data.table>) AcsTable shape (see census_shapes): one row per geography, structural spine plus one column per requested variable typed *E/*M rule; promise thereof.

title: CensusACS: American Community Survey aggregates — CensusACS
text: Retrieve entire ACS table (variable group) one call via API's group(...) selector — whole *E/*EA/*M/*MA quartet every line table, plus GEO_ID NAME.

code: CensusACS$get_acs_group(   year,   dataset = "acs1",   group,   geo_for = "us",   geo_in = NULL,   validate_geo = TRUE )

Default TRUE.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ acs <- CensusACS$new() # Population and median household income for every California county: ca <- acs$get_acs(   2023, "acs1",   variables = c("NAME", "B01001_001E", "B19013_001E"),   geo_for = "county:*", geo_in = "state:06" ) # A whole table at once with the group() convenience: income <- acs$get_acs_group(2023, "acs1", group = "B19013", geo_for = "state:*")  # Asynchronous: acs_async <- CensusACS$new(async = TRUE) main <- coro::async(function() {   dt <- await(acs_async$get_acs(2023, "acs1", c("NAME", "B01001_001E"), geo_for = "state:*"))   print(dt) }) main() while (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now() } # }

what: CensusBase: Abstract Base Class for Census API Clients — CensusBase
title: CensusBase: Abstract Base Class for Census API Clients — CensusBase
text: shared base every Census R6 client extends. provides transport plumbing (single sync/async request funnel, retry, throttle) inheriting connectcore::RestClient, customises two seams specific Census Data API.

title: CensusBase: Abstract Base Class for Census API Clients — CensusBase
text: two overridden private seams : .sign() — Census authentication single query-parameter key, appends &key=<CENSUS_API_KEY> request (via internal census_sign_key()). HMAC JWT; timestamp context unused. .parse_envelope() — Census envelope (via internal parse_census_response()): detects missing/invalid-key HTML page (arrives HTTP 200 httr2 follows redirect), text/plain API error bodies, empty-body -data case, otherwise parses array--arrays.

title: CensusBase: Abstract Base Class for Census API Clients — CensusBase
text: async argument selects execution mode every method: async = FALSE (default): methods return data.table::data.table. async = TRUE: methods return promises::promise resolves data.table. mode stored private$.is_async threaded every method's connectcore::then_or_now(res, ..., is_async = private$.is_async) tail; nothing hardcodes . Consume promises coro::async() await(); drive loop script (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now().

title: CensusBase: Abstract Base Class for Census API Clients — CensusBase
text: key read CENSUS_API_KEY environment variable (api_key argument overrides). empty key warns construction rather aborting – caller can still introspect metadata – data query empty (invalid) key aborts typed census_conditions error request time. class meant instantiated directly; subclasses (e.g. CensusEconomicIndicators) define public methods.

title: CensusBase: Abstract Base Class for Census API Clients — CensusBase

what: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: Retrieves US Census Bureau's Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — national economic prints advance retail sales (MARTS), business formation (BFS), advance durable-goods orders (ADVM3), new residential construction (RESCONST). Every program queried way returns tidy EitsSeries shape.

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: EITS predicate-driven grammar: fixed variable list always requested, query narrowed predicates – time (required), category_code (industry/segment), data_type_code (sales vs inventory, level vs change), seasonally_adj. NULL predicate returns every value dimension. result long/tidy: one row per (period x category x data_type x adjustment). EITS API serves national (us) geography ; geo_for defaults "us" rarely needs changing. wire keys /reserved words R, arguments named geo_for/geo_in (geo_for used , EITS nested geography). Inherits CensusBase; methods honour async flag set construction (sync returns data.table, async promise).

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: Census API returns latest revised value period — vintage/-parameter. Retail sales, business formation, construction heavily revised, naive historical pull current-vintage look-ahead trap backtest. Source revisable history ALFRED snapshot forward pulls fetch time; never feed API's revised history point--time model.

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: connectcore::RestClient -> census::CensusBase -> CensusEconomicIndicators

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: census::CensusBase$initialize()

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: CensusEconomicIndicators$get_series() CensusEconomicIndicators$get_business_formation() CensusEconomicIndicators$get_retail_advance() CensusEconomicIndicators$get_durable_goods_advance() CensusEconomicIndicators$get_housing_starts() CensusEconomicIndicators$clone()

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: Retrieve EITS program's tidy series, filtered given predicates. time required (API rejects query without ); accepts year ("2024", periods), month ("2024-03"), range ("2020 2024"). NULL category_code / data_type_code / seasonally_adj returns every value dimension.

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
code: CensusEconomicIndicators$get_series(   program,   category_code = NULL,   data_type_code = NULL,   time = NULL,   seasonally_adj = NULL,   geo_for = "us" )

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: program (scalar<character>) EITS program code, one names(EITS_PROGRAMS), e.g. "marts". category_code (scalar<character> | NULL) industry/segment predicate; NULL returns categories. data_type_code (scalar<character> | NULL) item-type predicate; NULL returns data types. time (scalar<character> | NULL) required ISO time predicate: year, month, "... ..." range. NULL aborts validation error naming requirement. seasonally_adj (scalar<character c("yes", "")> | NULL) adjustment filter; NULL returns . geo_for (scalar<character>) geography clause (wire key ); defaults "us" (geography EITS serves).

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: (EitsSeries | promise<EitsSeries>) tidy series, promise thereof.

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
text: Business Formation Statistics (bfs): weekly monthly business-application counts, released ~5 days week-end (weekly) ~11-12 days month-end (monthly); monthly release revises prior two months. convenience wrapper get_series("bfs", ...).

title: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) — CensusEconomicIndicators
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ eits <- CensusEconomicIndicators$new() # All categories/data-types of advance retail sales for 2024: marts <- eits$get_series("marts", time = "2024") # A single retail series, seasonally adjusted, over a multi-year range: retail <- eits$get_series(   "marts",   category_code = "44000", data_type_code = "SM",   seasonally_adj = "yes", time = "from 2019 to 2024" ) # Convenience wrappers pin the program: bfs <- eits$get_business_formation(time = "2024")  # Asynchronous: eits_async <- CensusEconomicIndicators$new(async = TRUE) main <- coro::async(function() {   marts <- await(eits_async$get_series("marts", time = "2024"))   print(marts) }) main() while (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now() } # }

what: The fixed EITS return-variable list — EITS_GET_VARS
title: The fixed EITS return-variable list — EITS_GET_VARS
text: Every EITS program serves fixed variable set, get_series() always requests list via get= clause filters predicates (time, category_code, data_type_code, seasonally_adj). time_slot_id included programs (e.g. RESCONST) require get= list even though surfaced returned shape.

title: The fixed EITS return-variable list — EITS_GET_VARS
code: EITS_GET_VARS

title: The fixed EITS return-variable list — EITS_GET_VARS
text: scalar<character>: comma-separated get= variable list.

what: EITS program codes served by the Census Data API — EITS_PROGRAMS
title: EITS program codes served by the Census Data API — EITS_PROGRAMS
text: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) programs addressable /data/timeseries/eits/<code>. name value coincide (code path segment); named list whitelist get_series() validates program argument .

title: EITS program codes served by the Census Data API — EITS_PROGRAMS
code: EITS_PROGRAMS

title: EITS program codes served by the Census Data API — EITS_PROGRAMS
text: named list scalar<character> path segments: bfs: Business Formation Statistics (weekly + monthly). marts: Advance Monthly Retail Trade (MARTS). mrts: Monthly Retail Trade (full). mwts: Monthly Wholesale Trade. mtis: Manufacturing Trade Inventories Sales. m3: Manufacturers' Shipments, Inventories, Orders (full M3). advm3: Advance Durable Goods (advance M3). ftd: International Trade Goods Services (full). ftdadv: Advance International Trade Goods. resconst: New Residential Construction (permits/starts). ressales: New Residential Sales. vip: Construction Spending (Value Construction Put Place). qss: Quarterly Services Survey. hv: Housing Vacancies Homeownership.

what: census: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API — census-package
title: census: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API — census-package
text: connector US Census Bureau Data API (api.census.gov), built shared connectcore::RestClient transport base. speaks Bureau's distinctive array--arrays wire format (header row followed string data rows) returns tidy data.table::data.tables.

what: What is covered in this version
title: census: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API — census-package
text: CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) family – retail sales (MARTS), business formation (BFS), durable goods (ADVM3), new residential construction (RESCONST), every EITS program via general get_series(). Keyless discovery: census_datasets(), census_variables(), census_geographies() introspect catalogue dataset metadata API key required. census_backfill_series(): standalone multi-year EITS pull.

title: census: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API — census-package
text: Every request-making surface supports synchronous mode (returns data.table) asynchronous mode (returns promises::promise resolving data.table), selected async argument. See CensusBase shared mechanism.

title: census: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API — census-package
text: Data queries require Census API key (free credential https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html); metadata/discovery endpoints . Supply CENSUS_API_KEY environment variable api_key constructor argument. newly issued key must activated (via confirmation email) data queries succeed; API redirects data queries HTML error page, package surfaces typed census_conditions error.

title: census: API Wrapper to the US Census Bureau Data API — census-package
text: Maintainer: Dereck Mezquita dereck@mezquita.io (ORCID)

Default NULL. base_url (scalar<character>) Census Data API base URL. Defaults census_base_url(). async (scalar<logical>) TRUE, returns promise. Default FALSE.

text: (VariableDictionary | promise<VariableDictionary>) one row per matched variable, promise thereof.

text: Fetches dataset's variables.json (source census_variables()) returns rows requested variables; NULL returns whole dictionary. Use attach meaning get_acs() result — e.g. join name table's column names.

what: Read the Census API key from the environment — census_api_key
title: Read the Census API key from the environment — census_api_key
text: Resolves CENSUS_API_KEY environment variable, returning empty string unset. empty string valid (keyless) state metadata endpoints; data queries empty key abort typed census_conditions error request time.

title: Read the Census API key from the environment — census_api_key
code: census_api_key()

title: Read the Census API key from the environment — census_api_key

what: Backfill an ACS variable set across survey years — census_backfill_acs
title: Backfill an ACS variable set across survey years — census_backfill_acs
text: Pulls ACS variables geography across range survey years stacks one long table, adding year column distinguish vintages. thin loop CensusACS's get_acs(), one request per year.

title: Backfill an ACS variable set across survey years — census_backfill_acs
code: census_backfill_acs(   from,   to,   dataset = "acs1",   variables,   geo_for = "us",   geo_in = NULL,   api_key = census_api_key(),   base_url = census_base_url(),   sleep = 0 )

title: Backfill an ACS variable set across survey years — census_backfill_acs
text: (scalar<count [2005, Inf[>) first survey year (inclusive). (scalar<count [2005, Inf[>) last survey year (inclusive). dataset (scalar<character>) ACS dataset, "acs1" "acs5". variables (vector<character, 1..>) get= variables (max 50). geo_for (scalar<character>) geography clause; default "us". geo_in (scalar<character> | NULL) clause; default NULL. api_key (scalar<character>) Census API key. Defaults CENSUS_API_KEY environment variable. base_url (scalar<character>) Census Data API base URL. Defaults census_base_url(). sleep (scalar<numeric [0, Inf[>) seconds pause years. Default 0.

title: Backfill an ACS variable set across survey years — census_backfill_acs
text: (data.table) stacked multi-year AcsTable year column (empty data.table year column year returned data).

title: Backfill an ACS variable set across survey years — census_backfill_acs
text: ACS year separate dataset (time predicate), iterates :requests year turn. year Bureau release (e.g. standard acs1 2020) returns HTTP error; year skipped warning rather aborting whole backfill. geography's requires chain validated front (per year). result dynamic-width AcsTable prepended year (integer) column; column presence still follows requested variables.

title: Backfill an ACS variable set across survey years — census_backfill_acs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ income <- census_backfill_acs(   from = 2018, to = 2023, dataset = "acs1",   variables = c("NAME", "B19013_001E"), geo_for = "state:*" ) } # }

what: Backfill a multi-year EITS series — census_backfill_series
title: Backfill a multi-year EITS series — census_backfill_series
text: Pulls EITS program's history across range years one call, returning single tidy EitsSeries table. thin loop CensusEconomicIndicators's get_series(), one request per year, results deduplicated sorted.

title: Backfill a multi-year EITS series — census_backfill_series
code: census_backfill_series(   program,   from,   to,   category_code = NULL,   data_type_code = NULL,   seasonally_adj = NULL,   geo_for = "us",   api_key = census_api_key(),   base_url = census_base_url(),   sleep = 0 )

title: Backfill a multi-year EITS series — census_backfill_series
text: program (scalar<character>) EITS program code, one names(EITS_PROGRAMS), e.g. "marts". (scalar<count [1900, Inf[>) first year pull (inclusive). (scalar<count [1900, Inf[>) last year pull (inclusive). category_code (scalar<character> | NULL) industry/segment predicate; NULL returns . data_type_code (scalar<character> | NULL) item-type predicate; NULL returns . seasonally_adj (scalar<character c("yes", "")> | NULL) adjustment filter; NULL returns . geo_for (scalar<character>) geography clause; default "us". api_key (scalar<character>) Census API key. Defaults CENSUS_API_KEY environment variable. base_url (scalar<character>) Census Data API base URL. Defaults census_base_url(). sleep (scalar<numeric [0, Inf[>) seconds pause yearly requests. Default 0.

title: Backfill a multi-year EITS series — census_backfill_series
text: (EitsSeries) deduplicated, sorted multi-year series.

title: Backfill a multi-year EITS series — census_backfill_series
text: function constructs synchronous client api_key / base_url, requests time = "<year>" year [, ], sleeping sleep seconds requests. Rows deduplicated (datetime, category_code, data_type_code, seasonally_adj) sorted. Narrow pull category_code / data_type_code / seasonally_adj specific series; leaving NULL returns every combination, wide program many years large table. Point--time caveat: Census API returns latest-revised values, backfill current-vintage – look-ahead trap backtest. See CensusEconomicIndicators mitigation.

title: Backfill a multi-year EITS series — census_backfill_series
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ retail <- census_backfill_series(   "marts",   from = 2015, to = 2024,   category_code = "44000", data_type_code = "SM", seasonally_adj = "yes" ) } # }

title: Typed census conditions — census_conditions
text: census raises classed conditions caller branches error type reads structured fields instead matching message text.

title: Typed census conditions — census_conditions
text: Transport failures nest specific -> general census_api_error_<status> -> census_api_error -> connectcore_api_error_<status> -> connectcore_api_error -> connectcore_error, carrying fields status, url, body_snippet, key_error. Raised non-2xx HTTP status, text/plain API error body, missing/invalid-key HTML page. Validation failures nest census_validation_error -> census_error (domain root). Raised bad program, --range argument, construction-time credential problem, request made. missing/invalid-key case special: API answers keyless bad-key data query HTTP 302 redirect missing_key.html / invalid_key.html, httr2 follows redirect, arrives HTTP 200 HTML body. envelope parser detects (final URL, content type) synthesises census_api_error_401 carrying key_error = TRUE, callers can catch 401 uniformly read message names activation requirement. url stored query-string credentials redacted (via connectcore::scrub_url(), lists key first sensitive-parameter set), logging e$url never leaks key.

title: census return shapes — census_shapes
text: Reusable roxyassert @type shapes parsed census data.tables. Measurement columns (published statistic value) typed | NA; structural columns (codes, identifiers, period timestamps, flags) strict. census leaf connector: nothing internal calls per-shape validator downstream package validates shapes, @genassert (callable validators generate) @exportassert (nothing export); shapes exist expanded function's return contract.

title: census return shapes — census_shapes
text: get_acs() / get_acs_group() return dynamic-width data.table — one row per geography, whose columns depend variables requested geography queried — documented shape rather pinned fixed column contract (method's @return (data.table | promise<data.table>), data.table class asserted boundary). worked example get_acs(2023, "acs1", c("NAME", "B01001_001E", "B19013_001E"), geo_for = "county:*", geo_in = "state:06") columns : name (character | NA) geography label, NAME variable, e.g. "Los Angeles County"; structural. b01001_001e (numeric | NA) total-population estimate (*E estimate variable); measurement. b19013_001e (numeric | NA) median-household-income estimate; measurement. state (character) state FIPS code, e.g. "06"; geography-code column, structural. county (character) county FIPS code, e.g. "037"; geography-code column, structural. typing rule dynamic columns (applied parser header name, checking annotation suffixes first): *EA / *MA annotation-flag columns (character | NA). *E / *M (profile *PE / *PM) estimate margin--error columns (numeric | NA); Bureau's suppression/annotation sentinels (large negative "jam" values) kept verbatim, coerced NA. NAME, GEO_ID, string variables, every geography-code column (state, county, tract, block group, ...) (character | NA); geography-code columns always populated practice. census_backfill_acs() prepends year (integer) column, survey vintage, distinguish stacked years.

title: List a dataset's variables (keyless) — census_variables
text: (VariableDictionary | promise<VariableDictionary>) one row per variable, promise thereof.

title: List a dataset's variables (keyless) — census_variables
text: Sourced dataset's variables.json. EITS program fixed measurement/predicate set (cell_value, category_code, ...); ACS vintage tens thousands estimate/margin variables. Use validate variable name data call.

title: census 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2017-10-26 Phase 2 — American Community Survey (ACS): demographic aggregates owner personally wanted. plain English: release adds survey behind official US income, employment, population figures every geography (state, county, city, right city block group), can ask “place look like” typed, tested way ask “retail sales move”. CensusACS: ACS aggregate client. get_acs() pulls set variables (50) geography wide table — one row per place, one column per variable, estimates margins numeric, annotations codes character. get_acs_group() pulls whole table via API’s group(...) selector. acs1 (1-year) acs5 (5-year); synchronous asynchronous. census_backfill_acs(): stacks variables across range survey years one table year column. survey year separate dataset, year Bureau never released (e.g. 1-year 2020) skipped warning rather aborting pull. census_acs_labels(): cheap keyless lookup human-readable labels set ACS variables, interpret wide table’s terse column codes. Geography validation requires chains, grounded live API’s real behaviour: wildcard child (county:*) may omit parents, fully-qualified one (county:037) must supply ; unknown level caught keyed call. Envelope fix: non-200 text/html body (404 unreleased survey year) now surfaced plain HTTP error rather mistaken missing/invalid-key page.

title: census 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2017-10-23 Initial release: US Census Bureau data fleet’s connector idiom — owner-commissioned “never know try” package. plain English: package fetches official US government statistics — many new businesses started week, retail sales moved month, many homes broke ground — one typed, tested interface works synchronously asynchronously, research future strategy can consume government data exactly way consumes exchange data. CensusEconomicIndicators: EITS time-series family — get_series() plus named helpers weekly Business Formation Statistics, advance retail report (MARTS), advance durable goods, housing starts; sync + async threaded constructor via connectcore. Keyless discovery layer: census_datasets(), census_variables(), census_geographies() — browse Bureau’s full catalogue without key. census_backfill_series(): standalone multi-year history pulls. Faithful typed shapes (EitsSeries et al.) every column documented typed bullets — cell_value numeric | NA Bureau legitimately suppresses cells; structural columns strict. Typed conditions birth (census_api_error connectcore chain; census_validation_error census_error), Bureau’s missing/invalid-key HTML redirect detected raised clear activation-naming error — test proving error ever leaks key material. Grounded live API activated key (20 live tests) fully-synthetic fixtures offline (134 tests); three design assumptions corrected reality (time required; predicate-echo duplicate columns; national geography level code).

title: census 0.0.1
text: Initial release: US Census Bureau Data API connector shared connectcore transport base. CensusBase: abstract R6 base connectcore::RestClient, plugging two Census seams — query-parameter key sign seam array--arrays envelope. envelope detects missing/invalid-key HTML page (arrives HTTP 200 httr2 follows redirect) surfaces typed census_api_error_401 naming key activation requirement. CensusEconomicIndicators: Economic Indicators Time Series (EITS) family. get_series() covers every EITS program (predicate-driven: time, category_code, data_type_code, seasonally_adj), get_business_formation(), get_retail_advance(), get_durable_goods_advance(), get_housing_starts() pinning market-moving programs. synchronous asynchronous (promise) modes. census_backfill_series(): standalone, instance-free multi-year EITS pull pages time predicate year--year, deduplicates, returns one tidy EitsSeries. Keyless discovery: census_datasets(), census_variables(), census_geographies() introspect ~1,790-dataset catalogue dataset’s variable/geography metadata API key. Typed conditions: census_api_error layered front connectcore transport chain, census_validation_error -> census_error (domain root). API keys redacted every stored URL via connectcore::scrub_url(). Fully synthetic mock fixtures exercising array--arrays parser (including null cell predicate-echoed duplicate columns), plus live-test battery gated CENSUS_LIVE_TESTS. built release (designed deferred): CensusACS (wide cross-sectional aggregates) CensusCPS (microdata).
